skill.name: security-review
skill.description: Guides AI coding assistants through web application security reviews, with checklists and examples for authentication, input validation, secrets, APIs, and deployment readiness.
skill.summary: Review application code for common web security risks before release.
skill.author: affaan-m

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network, environment, sensitive file, and obfuscation patterns in SKILL.md. Review found these are Markdown examples for a security checklist, not executable skill logic, but one approved CSP example recommends unsafe script directives that could weaken user applications if copied.

medium_findings.title: Unsafe Content Security Policy Guidance
medium_findings.description: The CSP example is presented as security guidance but includes unsafe script directives. Users may copy this pattern into production and reduce protection against cross-site scripting.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines appear under a Content Security Policy section and are not marked as dangerous. The unsafe directives are explicit and contradict the stated goal of secure guidance.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The external command and backtick detections occur inside Markdown code fences that demonstrate TypeScript templates, SQL strings, cookie headers, tests, and npm audit commands. No executable script files or runtime hooks were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static patterns are real text matches, but the surrounding context is Markdown instructional content. I found no evidence that the skill executes these commands automatically.

low_findings.title: Network Findings Are Relative Test Calls and Resource Links
low_findings.description: Fetch calls use relative API paths in example tests, and hardcoded URLs are public documentation links. No evidence found of data exfiltration or hidden outbound network behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The fetch examples target local application routes, and the external URLs are listed as resources. I found no credential-bearing outbound request in executable skill code.

low_findings.title: Environment And Sensitive Access Findings Are Safe Examples
low_findings.description: Environment variable and sensitive-file matches appear in examples that tell users to store secrets in environment variables and ignore local environment files. These are not direct attempts to read secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious strings are used in a secrets management checklist. The skill contains no code that reads .env files, browser credential stores, or operating system credential databases.

low_findings.title: Obfuscation And Heuristic Findings Are Contextual False Positives
low_findings.description: Base64 usage appears in a wallet signature verification example, not as hidden payload decoding. The dangerous-combination heuristic is triggered by unrelated examples across the same Markdown file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The base64 conversions are visible and tied to public key signature verification. The code execution, network, and credential references do not form a single executable flow.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unsafe Inline Script CSP Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes a CSP example with unsafe inline and eval script permissions. This is a dangerous configuration pattern when presented as recommended practice.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicitly present in recommended CSP guidance. It is not malicious, but it can materially weaken deployed application security.

dangerous_patterns.title: Intentionally Vulnerable Examples Require Careful Framing
dangerous_patterns.description: Several code blocks show hardcoded secrets, SQL concatenation, token localStorage, and sensitive logging as negative examples. They are clearly labeled as wrong or dangerous, so they are not confirmed malicious.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The vulnerable snippets are real, but the labels instruct users not to use them. The residual risk is accidental copying or poor model interpretation.

content.value_statement: Security mistakes in authentication, APIs, secrets, and input handling can create serious product risk. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured review checklist for common web application threats.
actual_capabilities: Provides a checklist for secrets, input validation, SQL injection, XSS, CSRF, and rate limiting.
Shows secure and insecure examples for authentication, cookies, logging, and error handling.
Guides review of Supabase row level security and role-based authorization controls.
Includes security testing examples for authentication, authorization, validation, and rate limits.
Offers a pre-deployment checklist for production web applications and sensitive features.
limitations: Does not run scanners, inspect dependencies, or verify live infrastructure by itself.
Some examples are framework-specific and may need adaptation for other stacks.
The CSP example should be revised before users copy it into production.
Cannot replace a manual security review for high-risk payment or authentication systems.

use_cases.title: Pre-release API Review
use_cases.description: Check new API endpoints for validation, authorization, rate limiting, and safe error responses before deployment.

use_cases.title: Authentication Feature Review
use_cases.description: Review login, session, token, cookie, and role logic before shipping user account features.

use_cases.title: Deployment Security Checklist
use_cases.description: Use a structured checklist to confirm secrets, headers, logs, dependencies, and database policies are ready for production.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Small Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the security-review skill to review this change. Focus on secrets, user input, authentication, authorization, and error handling. List concrete risks and fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Check a New API Endpoint
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the security-review skill to audit this API endpoint. Check input validation, SQL injection, authorization, CSRF, rate limiting, logging, and response safety.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Authentication Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the security-review skill to evaluate this authentication flow. Review token storage, cookie flags, session expiry, role checks, password handling, and account recovery risks.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Release Gate Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the security-review skill as a release gate. Review the code and deployment checklist, classify findings by severity, and provide blocking issues first.

output_examples.input: Review a new user creation endpoint before release.
output: High: The endpoint creates users without checking caller permissions.
Medium: The request body needs schema validation for email and role fields.
Low: Error messages should avoid exposing database constraint details.

output_examples.input: Check a login flow that stores tokens in browser storage.
output: High: Browser storage increases token exposure during cross-site scripting attacks.
Recommended fix: Use secure, httpOnly, SameSite cookies for session tokens.
Verification: Add tests for cookie flags and session expiry behavior.

output_examples.input: Prepare a production security checklist for a web app.
output: Confirm secrets are only in environment-managed storage.
Confirm API routes enforce authentication, authorization, validation, and rate limits.
Confirm logs, errors, and headers do not expose sensitive data.

best_practices: Ask for file paths and security boundaries before reviewing broad application changes.
Treat every finding as actionable by including impact, evidence, and a specific fix.
Use the checklist before deployment and after changes to authentication, payments, or secrets.
anti_patterns: Do not copy insecure examples into production code, even when they appear near safe examples.
Do not rely on this skill as the only review for regulated or high-risk systems.
Do not accept generic security advice without mapping it to the actual code path.

faq.question: Does this skill run automated security scanners?
faq.answer: No. It provides review guidance and examples, but it does not execute scanners or inspect dependencies automatically.

faq.question: Can it review authentication code?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers token handling, cookies, role checks, sessions, and authorization decisions.

faq.question: Does it support API security reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes checks for validation, injection risks, CSRF, rate limiting, logging, and error handling.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish as-is?
faq.answer: Not recommended. The skill should revise its CSP example before marketplace publication.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should users verify manually?
faq.answer: Users should verify live configuration, dependency scanner results, access controls, and production secrets outside the skill.
